MRND - modified radical neck dissection.
RND - radical neck dissection.
and examination. It is important to establish if any pre-
vious surgery has been performed, including the age of
tonsillectomy. Clinicians should be aware that previous
tonsillectomy does not preclude the possibility of a ton-
sillar remnant and this harbouring SCC. Therefore, a
high index of clinical suspicion should be exercised in
patients who had previous tonsillectomies and present
with potentially occult primary SCC of the head and
neck region.
Patients who present with a clinically obvious tonsillar
mass should undergo examination and biopsy under
general anaesthetic. The presence of malignant lateral
cervical lymphadenopathy in the absence of an obvious
index tumour represents a challenging diagnostic and
management dilemma [12,13]. In patients who have no
identifiable primary site a systematic approach with an
